page: The UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-8GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and is equipped with 40 GB of local SSD storage. It features an x86_64 architecture with support for advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, vector neural network instructions (AVX512_VNNI), and SHA cryptography extensions, alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-threaded performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU execution, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top 10% tier. However, multi-threaded scaling is constrained by the shared CPU allocation and 2-core limit, as evidenced by average multi-core stress-ng results and a weak rating for multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 397.31 GB/sec, the overall PassMark memory score and database operations fall into the bottom 25% tier.

This resource profile is suitable for lightweight general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and basic caching systems, with static web throughput reaching an average of 9.71 GB/sec and Redis SET operations performing at 5.88 million operations per second. It is not suited for intensive database operations or medium-to-large language model inference, where prompt processing for a 7B model drops to a poor rating of 14.06 tokens/sec. The shared CPU allocation offers a cost-effective entry point for development environments and low-traffic applications, trading off sustained multi-threaded compute guarantees for budget efficiency.
description: The UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-8GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor at 2.0 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM and 40 GB of local SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it supports AVX-512 and SHA extensions. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-threaded scaling and memory performance remain in the average to weak tiers due to the shared CPU allocation. Static web serving and Redis operations perform at average levels, while database operations and larger LLM inference tasks show clear performance limitations. This server is best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, static web hosting, and development environments where budget efficiency is prioritized over sustained multi-threaded compute resources.
og_description: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-8GB is a KVM virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 40 GB SSD. It offers top-tier single-core performance but is limited in multi-threaded and database workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-8GB is a KVM virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 40 GB SSD, designed for budget-friendly general workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining strong single-core AMD EPYC performance with local SSD storage for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory
40 GB of local SSD storage and 1 public IPv4 address included
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Reduced multi-threaded scaling and database performance due to shared CPU resources
Suited for static web serving, Redis caching, and development environments
